title: Uddhav Thackeray seeks disqualification of rebel MPs, says defections part of bigger conspiracy
content: MPs loyal to the Shiv Sena (UBT) had submitted a formal representation to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, seeking an opportunity to present their side, Uddhav Thackeray said, noting that party MP Arvind Sawant had cut short an official visit to Kargil after the Speaker had advanced the date of the hearing

title: Kerala government replaces prosecutor in ED assault case amid bail controversy, appoints Special Public Prosecutor
content: Police allege that the prosecutor failed to effectively oppose the bail application, despite a detailed report recommending its rejection

title: Tiruppur woman dies after botched YouTube-assisted childbirth at home
content: K. Sasikala, of Punjaithalavaipalayam in Uthukuli taluk, was taken to a private hospital in Coimbatore, where she died on Sunday morning of post-partum haemorrhage and related complications

title: Veteran journalist and filmmaker N.S. Shankar passes away
content: N.S. Shankar left a lasting mark on the Kannada cultural scene. He is recognised as a journalist and also for his breakthrough movie as a director ‘Ulta Palta’

title: From seas to skies, India is becoming secure, self-reliant: PM Modi
content: Prime Minister Modi highlights indigenously built missile systems and C-295 aircraft, says June was a “landmark month” for India’s defence sector; he thanks people for responding to his call for austerity amid the West Asia crisis

title: Revanth challenges Opposition to Assembly debate on his govt. performance
content: Chief Minister praises Nalgonda MLA and Minister Komatireddy Venkat Reddy for his role in Telangana movement

title: Bidadi project provides ground for Vokkaliga leaders’ one-upmanship
content: The political narrative has become messier with personal allegations going back and forth between Shivakumar and Kumaraswamy

title: 45,966 children administered Pulse Polio drops in Wayanad
content: The DMO says 88% of the eligible children in the district were covered on the first day of the campaign

title: CPI(M) protests inauguration of rafting event during search for missing person
content: The party alleges that inaugurating the rafting event in a celebratory manner while an intensive search for the missing person was under way nearby was inhuman and shocking

title: Elephant killings continue in death zone of Kerala
content: Human-induced elephant deaths in the Parambikulam-Munnar–Malayattoor forest sector are back in focus after the death of a makhna elephant in the Malayattoor forests, reportedly after chewing on fruit stuffed with explosives

title: Commerce Ministry convenes stakeholders' meet on June 30 on SEZ issues
content: The meeting will focus on issues related to the harmonisation of export promotion schemes and SEZ reforms, an official said

title: Karnataka Chief Minister lays foundation stone for Hebbal short tunnel project
content: The development comes even as civic groups and activists have criticised the project, calling it an unnecessary expenditure

title: Protest against ‘bid to appropriate’ Basava philosophy into Hindutva
content: The protesting groups have adopted three resolutions, including the demand for constitutional recognition for Lingayat as a religion

title: New portal for social auditing of rural schemes launched
content: Union Rural Development Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan launches the ‘Rural Internal Audit Portal’, a unified digital platform for end-to-end management of internal audits covering both risk-based and compliance audits

title: Young athlete’s organs will give others a second chance at life
content: A postgraduate from St. Thomas College, budding actor, and Kho Kho player declared brain dead after road accident; family chooses organ donation

title: Over 250 CRPF officers get maiden promotion after 15 years
content: Their much-delayed elevation came about after prolonged litigation and conditional resolution of pending service issues related to seniority and vacancies

title: KSRTC Budget Tourism Cell rolls out Hyderabad and monsoon packages
content: Pilgrimage circuit is scheduled to kick off on July 4 with a temple tour covering Guruvayur, Mammiyoor, Punnathur Kotta, Thriprayar, Kodungallur, and the Cherthala Karthyayani temple, priced at ₹1,360

title: Mamata loyalists file police complaint against ‘misuse’ of party name, symbol by rebels
content: Trinamool Congress faction led by Mamata Banerjee writes to Commissioner of Police, Kolkata, seeking permission to observe ‘Shahid Divas (Martyrs’ Day)’ on July 21 in the city’s Esplanade area.

title: SIT traces Sai Krishna’s custody trail from Markapuram to Vijayawada
content: Investigators question Task Force personnel, Krishnalanka police staff and senior officers as probe into alleged custodial death and destruction of evidence gathers pace; High Court to hear habeas corpus petition on Monday

title: Telangana rolls out Pulse Polio campaign, vaccinates 38.95 lakh children on first day
content: Campaign recorded a strong response on the opening day, with Health dept/ administering the vaccine to 38.95 lakh children through booth-based immunisation, covering 95.1% of the target population of 40.97 lakh

title: AHINDA leadership failed to meet expectations of backward classes, says seer
content: He was speaking after inaugurating a two-day State-level conference of the Ati Hindulida Mathadheeshara Mahasabha in Kalaburagi

title: Karnataka survey flags widespread dissatisfaction with telecom grievance redressal, seeks stronger TRAI action
content: Among the major grievances reported, network-related issues topped the list. This was followed by spam calls, billing disputes, and other quality-of-service complaints.

title: Kerala Agricultural University V-C appointment reflection of UDF-BJP deal: employees’ association
content: Unlike in other universities, Kerala Agricultural University Act stipulates that Chancellor shall appoint a temporary Vice-Chancellor only after consultation with the Pro-Chancellor, a position held by the Agriculture Minister, according to Confederation of University Employees Organisations

title: Andhra Pradesh issues excise policy guidelines for airport bars, liquor shops
content: New framework permits 24x7 operations, links Retail Excise Tax for bars to passenger traffic, and excludes Tirupati Airport from the licensing regime

title: Three students die, three injured as building roof collapses in Deodurg
content: The sajja (part of roof) of a shopping complex collapsed on them when they were sticking posters of a short film they had shot

title: Speaker refutes claims over silence on LDF liquor policy
content: Thiruvanchoor says he had strongly objected to the proposal to reduce taxes on liquor when it came up before the Assembly Subject Committee in 2022

title: Disciplinary action against protocol officials after Pinarayi misses flight from New Delhi
content: The Leader of the Opposition missed the flight to Karipur on Saturday after protocol officials allegedly failed to update the airline about his travel plan, even though he had reached the airport hours before departure

title: 'SUV, Rs 65 lakh house': Lavish lifestyle of key accused in Ram Temple 'donation theft' case
content: Investigators are scrutinizing the financial dealings and assets of Anukalp Mishra, a key suspect in the alleged embezzlement of Ram Temple donations. Police are examining his reportedly improved lifestyle, recent property acquisitions, and a lavish religious event to establish a money trail. His brother-in-law, Lavkush Mishra, also arrested, is under similar financial scrutiny.

title: Why India's AMCA still needs a US engine, and so do many of the world's fighters
content: India's ambitious fifth-generation fighter jet program faces a significant hurdle as the cost of crucial US-made engines has reportedly tripled, jeopardizing timelines and budgets. This reliance on foreign propulsion systems, also impacting the Tejas Mk2, highlights a persistent challenge in developing world-class fighter jet engines domestically. While other nations also depend on foreign engines initially, India's pursuit of strategic autonomy hinges on mastering this complex technology.

title: Khamenei says US-Israel ‘boasting’ over Iran strikes amounts to confession of crimes
content: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has demanded legal action against US and Israeli leaders for alleged war crimes, citing the deaths of schoolchildren in Minab. He views their public statements as admissions of guilt, urging domestic and international courts to pursue accountability. This comes as Iran's foreign ministry condemned recent US airstrikes on its southern coast, with both nations exchanging retaliatory actions and accusations.

title: Ben Stokes announces retirement from Tests, shocks cricket world
content: The announcement came while Stokes was in the middle of a bowling spell. England Cricket released a statement confirming that the 35-year-old would step away from international cricket after the conclusion of the Test.

title: How Telegram’s NEET controversy reveals a bigger battle over coaching, piracy and access
content: Telegram's temporary ban, linked to NEET exam leaks, sparks debate on its role in India's shadow education economy. While students utilize its features for affordable learning, the platform also facilitates the free circulation of paid educational content, leading to copyright battles. Critics argue the ban punishes legitimate users and fails to address the root cause of leaks, highlighting a complex challenge for policymakers balancing intellectual property with educational access.

title: Jeff Bezos vs Zohran Mamdani debate: Kevin O’Leary says Amazon founder should run cities
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. Meanwhile, Bezos also dismissed fears of AI causing job losses, predicting a labor shortage instead.

title: Nasa launches high-stakes mission to save ageing telescope Swift from falling to Earth
content: The mission will be carried out by US startup Katalyst Space Technologies, which won the Nasa contract last September. Its three-armed robotic spacecraft, named Lift, will launch aboard an air-launched Pegasus rocket from the Marshall Islands before spending about a month chasing down Swift in orbit. Swift has been observing the universe since 2004.

title: Michael Dell updates on Dell's Texas move days after Abbott's 'welcome home' message
content: Dell Technologies is officially moving its legal base from Delaware to Texas, with 97% of shareholders approving the change. CEO Michael Dell expressed pride in returning the company's legal home to the state where it was founded. This move aligns with Texas's growing appeal for major corporations, including Tesla, Coinbase, and Exxon Mobil, due to its favorable business climate and talent pool.

title: 13-year-old 'raped' by over 12 men for 4 days in Rajasthan; 10 arrested
content: A 13-year-old girl was allegedly sexually exploited by nearly a dozen individuals over four days in Sriganganagar, sparking outrage. The victim was reportedly taken to multiple hotels after seeking a ride home. Police have arrested 10 people, including hotel staff and an e-rickshaw driver, and are investigating further. DVR footage from four hotels is being analyzed to identify more suspects.

title: Land donated for $10 as a park sold for $10 million to build a data centre
content: A Texas city sold 87 acres donated in 1999 for a $10 public park to a data center developer for $10 million. Residents, led by Pamela Griffin, are suing to honour the original deed, while Taylor officials say existing zoning permits the data center without resident approval. The project promises $30 million in tax revenue but raises concerns over noise, water use and property values.

title: Drama! Smriti, Jemimah involved in heated staredown - Watch
content: Tempers flared during India's Women's T20 World Cup clash against Australia after a mix-up between close friends Smriti Mandhana and Jemimah Rodrigues led to Mandhana's run-out. The pair exchanged angry looks before Mandhana later brushed off the incident. Meanwhile, captain Harmanpreet Kaur's explosive 56 off 27 balls powered India to a competitive total of 170/4.

title: Odisha: Four minors attack catering staff for not serving mutton curry at wedding feast
content: Four teenagers were arrested in Odisha's Balasore district for allegedly assaulting a catering staff member. The incident occurred at a wedding feast when the victim reportedly refused to serve mutton, offering chicken instead. The dispute escalated, leading to the minors allegedly attacking the caterer with a sharp weapon, causing severe injuries.

title: Skydiving tragedy in France: Five students among 11 killed in plane crash
content: A source close to the investigation said the victims were part of a group of nursing students. The aircraft, which was registered in Germany, crashed into a grassy area near the runway at Nancy-Essey Aerodrome in eastern France. The crash site was located close to a residential neighbourhood and two roads, according to an AFP journalist at the scene.

title: 'Sentiments hurt': Ayodhya lawyers refuse to defend Ram temple donation case accused
content: Eight individuals accused in the Ayodhya Ram Temple donation case face a significant hurdle as local lawyers have unanimously agreed not to represent them. The Faizabad Bar Association is set to formalize this decision, citing hurt sentiments over the alleged theft of temple offerings. Meanwhile, police conducted raids on the accused's residences, with investigators having recovered nearly Rs 80 lakh.

title: Kerala government orders probe after protocol lapse causes Vijayan to miss flight
content: Kerala's opposition leader, Pinarayi Vijayan, missed his flight from Delhi to Kerala due to a protocol lapse. Officials failed to confirm travel plans with the airline, despite Vijayan reaching the airport well in advance. An inquiry has been ordered, with disciplinary action promised against the responsible protocol staff. Vijayan eventually travelled to Kannur on a later flight.

title: NFL trade rumors: Buffalo Bills linked with $6 million Denver Broncos WR ahead of 2026 season
content: The Buffalo Bills have emerged as a potential landing spot for Denver Broncos wide receiver Marvin Mims Jr. , whose future is uncertain after Denver acquired Jaylen Waddle. With Josh Allen entering his ninth NFL season, Buffalo could look to add another explosive playmaker. Mims admitted frustration with his role but remains professional, while analysts believe his speed and upside make him an intriguing trade candidate ahead of the 2026 season.

title: 'It's about creating spaces for girls': NBA’s long game beyond superstars
content: From the WPL to the WNBA, women's sport has entered a new era of growth. Yet, for Lauren Jackson, the bigger challenge is ensuring girls stay in sport long enough to become players, coaches and leaders. Speaking on the NBA's Her Time To Play leadership panel in Singapore, the Australian great argued that sustaining women's sport begins long before the professional stage.

title: Father 'forgives' girlfriend who kicked his 18-month-old toddler to death, sparks outrage in China
content: A father in China has sparked national fury by forgiving his girlfriend accused of fatally kicking their 18-month-old son. The child's mother, the ex-wife, demanded a thorough investigation after discovering the toddler's severe abdominal injuries, which led to his death. Authorities revealed the girlfriend allegedly inflicted the fatal blows, while the father claims he believed she was caring for the child.

title: Female cadet pilot injured by running propeller at Kanpur’s flight training institute
content: A cadet pilot from Delhi sustained a lower back injury after being hit by a propeller while disembarking a training aircraft in Kanpur. The incident occurred when the 22-year-old reportedly exited the Tecnam P2006T before both engines were fully shut down. The DGCA has launched an inquiry, the instructor has been grounded, and the aircraft is out of service pending investigation.

title: 14 killed as Saudi Aramco helicopter crashes in Ras Tanura
content: Tragedy struck Saudi Arabia's eastern city of Ras Tanura on Sunday when a Saudi Aramco helicopter crashed, claiming the lives of fourteen individuals. The cause of the devastating incident remains under investigation by authorities. Saudi Aramco, a global leader in oil production, operates significant facilities in Ras Tanura, a vital hub for the kingdom's crude oil exports.

title: ‘Look inwards’: India tears into Pakistan over Karachi blast allegations
content: India has firmly rejected Pakistan's claims linking it to the recent Karachi terrorist attack. The Ministry of External Affairs urged Islamabad to address its internal terror infrastructure and cease using terrorism as a state policy, rather than making baseless accusations. The incident involved militants attacking a paramilitary headquarters, resulting in casualties. Pakistan has vowed retaliation and previously conducted strikes in Afghanistan, which Kabul denies.

title: Sooryavanshi misses out again as India hand debuts to Prince and Suryansh
content: India aims to level the T20I series against Ireland after a surprising loss in the opener. Captain Shreyas Iyer has introduced two debutants, Suryansh Shedge and Prince Yadav, to the playing XI. The team is looking to bounce back and secure a victory in the second match of the series, with Shedge and Yadav eager to make their mark on the international stage.

title: The price of paradise: What happens when offbeat travel destinations go mainstream
content: Once a cherished secret, India's offbeat travel destinations are rapidly becoming mainstream. What began as a quiet escape for a few now faces overcrowding, mirroring global trends. As infrastructure improves and social media fuels aspiration, the very charm of these places is threatened. Travelers grapple with the desire for authentic experiences versus their role in accelerating discovery and its consequences.

title: ‘Poison or push?’: Siya Goyal, her ‘lover’ Chetan searched online for ways to kill Ketan, say cops
content: A chilling murder plot unfolded in Pune as a couple, Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary, meticulously planned realtor Ketan Agrawal's death. Online searches for murder methods and rehearsed attempts at Lohagad Fort reveal the premeditated nature of the crime. Driven by social pressures and a desire to prevent an arranged marriage, the duo executed their plan, making the fatal push appear accidental.

title: 'Age ko chhordiye': India-origin Ireland pacer Moondra makes Sooryavanshi admission
content: India-origin Ireland pacer Jai Moondra has backed teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, saying "age is just a number" and insisting the youngster has the potential to produce something special. Moondra's praise came after his dream debut for Ireland, where he claimed 2/25, including the wicket of Sanju Samson, helping Ireland register their first-ever international win over India.

title: Messi scripts history, breaks Brazil legend's FIFA WC record to become ...
content: The Argentina captain scored with a free-kick in the 80th minute at AT&T Stadium on Saturday (local time). It was his sixth World Cup goal from outside the box, taking him past Brazil great Rivelino, who had held the record with five.

title: Thar stunt, Fortuner chasing gazelle: Ladakh fines 4 in 1st crackdown on tourists
content: Ladakh's administration has taken unprecedented action against tourists for illegal off-roading in protected wildlife areas, including Pangong Lake. Four individuals faced hefty penalties totaling Rs 2 lakh and had their vehicles impounded for violating the Wildlife Protection Act. One offender was caught chasing a Tibetan gazelle within a sanctuary. This strict enforcement aims to safeguard Ladakh's fragile ecosystem and endangered wildlife, with authorities warning of continued stringent action against violators.

title: 'They thought it was a picnic': Ex-cricketer tears into Team India after Ireland shock
content: Former India opener Sadagoppan Ramesh has strongly criticized Team India's complacent approach following their unexpected 34-run loss to Ireland. He believes the team underestimated their opponents, prioritizing the upcoming England series over the T20I opener. Ramesh highlighted Ireland's smart execution, particularly with short-pitched bowling on larger grounds, and pointed to India's sloppy fielding and overconfidence as key factors in the shocking defeat.

title: Bengal braces for UCC battle as BJP government prepares to table Bill in assembly
content: West Bengal's BJP government is set to introduce a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill, following similar moves in other BJP-ruled states. Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari confirmed the legislation will be presented Monday, aiming to replace religion-based personal laws with a common framework. The BJP asserts tribal communities will be exempt, while the Trinamool Congress criticizes the move as political polarization, demanding extensive consultation.

title: 'Visit Ram Temple, do penance to gain good sense': UP CM Yogi to Akhilesh amid donation row
content: Uttar Pradesh chief minister Yogi Adityanath has sharply criticized Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav over alleged donation embezzlement in Ayodhya, urging him to visit the Ram Temple. Adityanath accused the previous SP government of firing on Ram devotees and diverting funds to graveyards instead of temples. Meanwhile, Yadav questioned Adityanath's frequent visits, suggesting they were to oversee alleged thefts and criticized Ayodhya's development quality.

title: 'He'll be tired': Vaughan spots one weakness in Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's game
content: Former England captain Michael Vaughan lauded 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for revolutionizing T20 batting, pushing par scores to 230. However, Vaughan highlighted fielding endurance as Sooryavanshi's key challenge at the international level, suggesting England exploit this by having India field first. Despite this, Sooryavanshi's impressive U19 World Cup and IPL performances showcase his potential.

title: Arunachal flash floods: Death toll rises to 3; IMD warns of over 200 mm of rainfall till Monday
content: Arunachal Pradesh grapples with rising flood death toll, now at three, after a missing man's body was recovered. Incessant rains triggered flash floods in Keyi Panyor district, displacing residents and damaging infrastructure. Rescue operations are ongoing, with NDRF personnel deployed to bolster efforts. Authorities have issued alerts for further heavy rainfall, warning of increased risks of landslides and waterlogging across multiple districts.

title: Venezuela quake: Race against time as rescuers battle rubble to find survivors; death toll tops 1400
content: Venezuela grapples with the aftermath of devastating twin earthquakes as the crucial 72-hour rescue window closes, dimming hopes for survivors. The death toll has tragically surpassed 1,430, with thousands injured and displaced. International aid and rescue teams are arriving, but damaged infrastructure, including hospitals, poses significant challenges, leaving an estimated 680,000 children in need of urgent assistance.

title: India's debt market not ready to fund next growth phase: Report
content: India's debt market requires urgent structural reforms to fund its ambitious economic growth, warns Deloitte. The report highlights that relying solely on bank deposits is no longer viable as household savings shift. To achieve its $7.3 trillion economy target by 2030, the market needs greater depth, liquidity, and integration to efficiently bridge the rising capital gap and support national economic aspirations.

title: Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw calls Bengaluru 'garbage city', sparks debate
content: Biocon founder Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw has voiced strong criticism regarding Bengaluru's urban development, lamenting the loss of its 'garden city' identity. She shared a stark visual comparison of current concrete-heavy infrastructure against a greener, more pedestrian-friendly alternative. Social media users echoed her concerns, attributing the city's decline to poor planning, corruption, and a lack of accountability from administrators and politicians.

title: Stokes angrily calls out Archer after pacer skips Bashir wicket celebration - Watch
content: England captain Ben Stokes was seen rallying Jofra Archer to join team celebrations during the third Test against New Zealand. After spinner Shoaib Bashir took a wicket, Archer initially stayed apart, seemingly frustrated by a dropped catch earlier. Stokes' decisive gesture emphasized team unity, drawing Archer back into the fold, a moment that resonated with fans praising his leadership.

title: Sonam Wangchuk begins indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar in support of CJP protest
content: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk has commenced an indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar, joining the Cockroach Janata Party's protest demanding Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. The demonstration, supported by students and farmer leaders, highlights alleged examination irregularities and calls for greater transparency and accountability in the education system. Concerns have been raised about potential house arrests of farmer leaders attempting to join the protest.

title: Iraqi security forces raid Baghdad Green Zone, arrest MPs in anti-corruption crackdown
content: Baghdad's high-security Green Zone saw a significant crackdown as Iraqi forces arrested politicians and officials in a sweeping anti-corruption drive. Judicial orders led to pre-dawn raids, targeting individuals implicated in financial corruption and potentially linked to smuggling and funding armed factions. This operation precedes Prime Minister Zaidi's US visit, signaling his commitment to reforms amidst ongoing investigations.

title: PM Modi Highlights Climate Impact On Island Nations In Seychelles
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Sunday said the Global South, especially island nations, was the most impacted by climate change and called for climate action guided by fairness, responsibility and equity, in his address to the National Assembly of Seychelles.

title: How A Rats' Nest Led To Poison Pills At Mumbai's Muharram Procession
content: Police sources said Premji had conducted extensive research on Google and AI regarding the effects of various dosages of zinc phosphide on the human body and had easy access to Zinc Phosphide.

title: '17 Years Of Silence': Baloch Activist's Open Letter Exposes Pak's Forced Disappearances
content: For years, Baloch nationalist groups, families of missing persons and international rights organisations have alleged that enforced disappearances have become a recurring feature of Pakistan's counter-insurgency operations

title: 50 kg Poison, 30,000 Pills, Iran Trips: Mumbai Accused's '15,000 Deaths' Plan
content: Fayyaz Premji's mother and sister live in Iran and Iraq. Over the last 12 months, he visited the two nations 19 times.

title: On Camera, 4 Members Of Family Killed As Speeding SUV Rams Car On UP Highway
content: Four members of the same family were killed and three other persons were left injured after an SUV crashed into the family's car in a tragic accident near Saharanpur.

title: 8 Bangladeshi Infiltrators Arrested At Tripura Airport
content: Tripura Police have arrested eight Bangladeshi nationals in two separate intelligence-led operations at Maharaja Bir Bikram Airport in the state capital, Agartala, foiling their suspected attempt to travel to other parts of the country.

title: China's New AI Cybersecurity Tool On Par With Anthropic's Mythos
content: Previewed in April, Mythos is designed to spot software vulnerabilities, yet experts warn it may enable stronger cyberattacks. This month, the US ordered Anthropic to stop exporting a less capable variant over national security concerns.

title: 1,000 More Deaths In France's Worst Heatwave, Total Rises To Over 4,000
content: Most of the fatalities involved older people and that it expected the mortality rate to rise as more information became available about deaths in residential care and homes.

title: The Hidden Story Behind Kim Jong Un's Mother And Her Secret Past
content: North Korea claims that the Kim family belongs to the sacred "Mount Paektu bloodline" - a lineage portrayed as pure, heroic and destined to rule the country.

title: Assam CID Cracks Down On Job Scam Racket; 7 Arrested
content: The accused persons, operating as part of an organised racket, allegedly cheated job-seeking youths of substantial sums of money.

title: MK Stalin's Son-In-Law Sends Notices To 2 TVK Ministers Over 'Defamation'
content: The notices, sent through Sabareesan's legal counsel, demand that the ministers withdraw their statements and issue an apology within 15 days, failing which legal action will be initiated.

title: 5-Year-Old Girl Dies After Falling From 10th-Floor Balcony In Delhi: Cops
content: A five-year-old girl died after allegedly falling from the 10th floor of a residential apartment building in southeast Delhi's Kalkaji Extension, police said on Sunday.

title: "Easier To Do It First, Then Tell Everybody": Shubhanshu Shukla's Mantra
content: Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla said he kept a journal throughout including audio notes, written logs and whatever was available on the advice of people who had done this before

title: 100x Surge In Wealth, Phone Data Erased: Fresh Details In Ram Temple Theft
content: The sources said the suspects attempted to delete digital evidence. They also tried to get rid of the proceeds of the crime.

title: Farmhouse, Scorpio, And More: Changed Lifestyle Of Ram Temple Theft Accused
content: Anukalp had managed to get his brother-in-law Lavkush appointed to the team that was tasked with counting the cash donated by devotees.

title: J&K To Add 200 New E-Buses To Fleet, Will Connect Rural And Tourist Areas
content: The procurement process of the buses is in advanced stages, and they will be inducted in Jammu and Srinagar cities in the next few months.

title: Police Case Against Bengal MLA Humayun Kabir Over Alleged Threats Against BJP
content: In the viral video, Humayun Kabir is heard saying that despite losing the election, the local BJP leaders - including Anamika Ghosh - are acting with the arrogance of elected representatives.

title: Seychelles Honours PM Modi With 'Guardian Of The Blue Horizon' Title
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was conferred with the honorary 'Guardian of the Blue Horizon' title by Seychelles for his steadfast commitment to environmental conservation and advancing the aspirations of the Small Island Developing States.

title: Putin admits 'difficult period' as Ukraine ramps up attacks deep inside Russia
